# Location Clustering — Workflow Review, Cleanup & Demo Script

*Screen references map to your upload order: Screen 1 \= landing, 2 \= Define Scope, 3 \= Select Signals, 4 \= Agent generating, 5 \= Choose a scenario, 6 \= Cross-cluster comparison, 7 \= Cluster fingerprint, 8 \= Manage stores.*

---

1

## ![][image1]

## PART A — Must-fix before you demo (these are credibility risks, not polish)

These will be caught by a numbers-literate retail audience and they attack your core message ("trust the math"). Fix the data or hide the screen.

1. **Store counts don't reconcile — three different totals.**  
     
   - Screen 1 header says **21 stores**, but the *Stores Assigned* KPI says **70** with the nonsensical subtext **"70 / 21 covered."** The cluster table below sums to 70 (18+15+16+13+8).  
   - Screens 5, 6, 8 show **20 stores** (4 per cluster ×5) and "20 STORES."  
   - Screen 2 scope and Screen 5 Scenario B both imply **21 stores** (Scenario B sums 5+8+6+2 \+1 more).  
   - **Fix:** pick one truth. Recommend a clean **21-store pilot → 20 assigned \+ 1 outlier**, with per-cluster counts that sum to 21\. Kill the "70" and "70/21 covered" entirely.

   

2. **Screen 6 (Cross-cluster comparison) is visibly broken.** Pro/DIY mix \= **NaN%**, Avg store size \= **0k**, Tile sales share \= **0%**, Velocity \= **0** across every cluster. Only Avg cohesion has real values. **Fix the calculations or do not open this tab live.** NaN on screen is the single most damaging thing you could show.  
     
3. **Cluster names don't carry through.** You accept **Scenario B — Behavioral**, whose clusters (Screen 5\) are *High-Velocity Pro Markets / Mid-Velocity Suburban / Volume-Value / Developing-Transitional*. But Screens 1, 6, 7, 8 then show *Pro-Heavy South / DIY-Heavy West / DIY-Heavy South / Mixed Urban East / Pro-Heavy Midwest*. **The accepted scenario's names must persist.** Pick one convention (the Pro/DIY \+ region hybrid is actually great for F\&D) and use it everywhere, including the Scenario B card.  
     
4. **Velocity contradicts itself on Screen 7\.** The "vs network" bar reads **Sales velocity −0.95** (below network), the radar shows velocity **near max**, and Cluster insights says **"Velocity A — faster than network median."** Three signals, two directions. **Fix the sign/encoding** so they agree (should be positive/faster).  
     
5. **Placeholder junk is showing.** Screen 4 run name \= **"asdfsdaf."** Screen 2 placeholder is truncated ("…network rur"). Screen 1 "70/21 covered." Clear all of these — sandbox cruft reads as "unfinished."  
     
6. **Run ID drift.** Screen 1 active set is **CR-018**; Screens 5–6 say **CR-019 · Live**. Decide which is live for the demo and keep it consistent end to end.

---

## PART B — Screen-by-screen: keep / cut / fix / enhance

### Screen 1 — Location Clustering (landing)

- **Keep:** the four-up KPI strip pattern, the store-distribution bar, the cluster table with dominant categories \+ SKU set, run history.  
- **Cut / demote:** **"Next re-run · Apr 12"** does not deserve a hero KPI slot — it's operational. Move it next to the *Re-run latest* button.  
- **Fix:** the *Stores Assigned* card (see A1).  
- **Enhance:** replace the freed KPI slot with an **insight** metric — e.g., **"Differentiation: 43-pt Pro spread"** (gap between most-Pro and least-Pro cluster) or **"Coverage: 20/21 assigned · 1 outlier."** That turns a status card into a talking point. In the cluster table, the *Pro Avg* (70%) should match the fingerprint's 71.3% — align rounding.

### Screen 2 — Step 1, Define Scope

- **Keep:** the 3-axis scope (Department · Channel · Season) and the right-rail *What happens next* — it primes a first-time user well.  
- **Cut:** in a live demo the *Tip* card is redundant with *What happens next*; collapse to one helper. **Online** as a clustering channel is odd for physical-store assortment — consider hiding it for F\&D.  
- **Enhance:** prefill the run name (no empty required field on stage) and show **"\~250 stores"** when scope \= All, so the 21 reads explicitly as a pilot, not the whole chain.

### Screen 3 — Step 2, Select Signals

- **Keep:** the three signal cards and the *Signal guidance* copy — clear and honest.  
- **Fix the conceptual gap:** you ask the user to pick **signals** (Performance / Demographics / Product attributes), but the agent then always generates the **same three scenarios** (Geographic / Behavioral / DC-based). Two of those three ignore the signals entirely. **Make the link explicit:** "Your signals power the **Behavioral** scenario; **Geographic** and **Operational** are always-on baselines to compare against." Otherwise a sharp user asks "why did you ask me to pick signals?"  
- **Enhance:** clarify **clustering signals vs. descriptive attributes** right here. You can cluster on Performance only yet still *describe* every cluster by Pro%, size, tile share (Screens 7–8). One line — "signals decide the grouping; we profile each cluster across all attributes" — pre-empts the "but I only picked Performance" confusion.

### Screen 4 — Step 3, Agent generating

- **Keep, and lean into it:** the **agent trace** (load profiles → normalize features \[0,1\] → cosine similarity → run k-means → score) is your single best "show the math" asset. It directly answers "what algorithm / what analysis."  
- **Fix:** "asdfsdaf" name; reconcile **\~12 seconds** est. runtime with the "couple of minutes" you say verbally — pick one and match it.  
- **Enhance:** make the trace steps the *exact* methodology you'll claim out loud, and surface the scoring dimensions here ("scoring composite · statistical fit · business actionability") so Screen 5's three numbers feel earned.

### Screen 5 — Step 3, Choose a scenario

- **Keep:** the three scenario cards with **Composite / Statistical / Business** scores and the **Agent recommended** badge on Behavioral — this *is* your "business \+ statistical inputs" story, and it shows the agent has a point of view while you decide.  
- **Fix:** header is overlapping the progress bar ("Choose a scenari… CR-01s") and the "Selected: Scenario B" label is clipped. Align Scenario B's cluster names with the rest (A3).  
- **Enhance (agentic):** add a one-line **"Why I recommend B"** from the agent — "highest composite (91), best balance of statistical fit and manageability." You have the raw line ("best balance of signal and manageability"); attribute it to the agent explicitly.

### Screen 6 — Cross-cluster comparison

- **Fix first (A2):** no NaN/0.  
- **Cut:** the **"Velocity (1=A)"** scalar row — encoding a letter band as a number reading "0" is confusing. Replace with **"% A-velocity stores"** per cluster.  
- **Enhance:** this is your "**stores across clusters**" view — make the differences obvious. Add **dominant categories** and **SKU-set size** as rows (you already show them on Screen 1), and sort/heat the row so the eye lands on where clusters diverge most (Pro/DIY mix and tile share for F\&D).

### Screen 7 — Cluster fingerprint

- **Keep & emphasize:** **Key drivers of inclusion** (Pro/DIY revenue split 78%, velocity tier 65%, category mix 52%) is your **explainability** crown jewel — it answers "why is this store in this cluster?" Make it more prominent, not a side panel.  
- **Cut the redundancy:** the **radar** and the **"vs network avg" bars** encode the same five dimensions twice. Keep **one** — I'd keep the bars (they carry the \+/− vs network, which is the insight) and shrink or drop the radar.  
- **Fix:** the velocity sign (A4) and make Cluster insights agree with the bars.

### Screen 8 — Manage stores

- **Keep:** this is your cleanest screen. The **FIT %** column (how well each store belongs), the add-store flow with **best-match suggestion** (Orlando Pro Center 91% fit), and the **Impact summary** are exactly right. This is your "**stores within a cluster**" view.  
- **Enhance (agentic):** make **Impact summary react live** — right now it says "no change" because nothing moved. When you remove San Antonio Pro or add Orlando, the cohesion/Pro%/avg-size should update in real time and the agent should comment ("cohesion \+0.02 — stronger fit"). That live recompute is the most convincing agentic moment in the whole module.

---

## PART C — The agentic angle (make the agent visible, and keep control obvious)

The agent already does the right things; surface them as moments:

1. **It shows its work** (Screen 4 trace) — narrate it; don't skip it.  
2. **It has a point of view** (Screen 5 "Agent recommended" Scenario B) — but **you accept**. Say "it recommends, you decide" out loud; that's the control message F\&D needs.  
3. **Use *Ask Alan*** (top-right copilot). A planted "Ask Alan — why Scenario B over A?" is a powerful explainability beat and shows the agent reasons in plain language. Pre-test the answer.  
4. **Live re-scoring on reassignment** (Screen 8\) — the agent reacting to a human override is the cleanest proof that human \+ agent co-pilot, not autopilot.  
5. **Close the loop:** state that accepting a scenario (Screen 5\) pushes it live and feeds every downstream decision (curation), and that store reassignments teach the next run. That's the "network learns" promise made concrete.

---

## PART D — Stages, steps & KPIs

**Stages/steps:** the **3-step wizard** (Define Scope → Select Signals → Review & Accept) is clean — keep it. One clarity fix: the wizard shows **3 steps** while the agent trace shows **"Step 4 of 6."** Label the trace as the agent's **pipeline** ("agent pipeline · 6 stages") so users don't think the wizard grew steps.

**KPIs to show (and where):**

- **Cluster-set health (landing):** Active clusters · Stores assigned \+ coverage/outliers · Avg cohesion (with the \>0.75 healthy marker — good) · Differentiation/Pro-spread.  
- **Per cluster (table \+ cross-cluster):** stores · Pro/DIY mix · cohesion · dominant categories · SKU-set size · % A-velocity · avg store size · **tile share** (F\&D-specific).  
- **Scenario quality (Screen 5):** Composite · Statistical fit (silhouette) · Business actionability — keep exactly.  
- **Within cluster (Screen 8):** per-store Pro% · size · velocity · cohesion contribution · **FIT %**.

**KPIs to drop:** the "Velocity (1=A)" scalar; raw "0k" sizes; the duplicate radar; "next re-run" as a hero metric; anything that can render NaN/0.

---

## PART E — Answers to have loaded for their likely questions

- **"What algorithm?"** → "k-means under the hood. The agent normalizes features to a common scale, builds a store-similarity matrix, runs the clustering, and **compares three framings — Geographic, Behavioral, Operational — scoring each on statistical fit and business actionability.** We recommend one; you accept." *(Screens 4 \+ 5.)*  
- **"What analysis backs it?"** → cohesion (how tight each cluster is), the scenario scores, the **key drivers of inclusion** (feature importance per cluster), and the **vs-network fingerprint.** *(Screens 5 \+ 7.)*  
- **"Stores within vs. across clusters?"** → within \= **Manage stores** with per-store FIT% (Screen 8); across \= **Cross-cluster comparison** (Screen 6, once fixed) \+ the distribution bar (Screen 1).  
- **"Which KPIs matter?"** → Part D set; for F\&D the decisive two are **Pro/DIY mix** and **tile share**.  
- **"What makes sense / what doesn't?"** → cluster on the signals that drive *behavior* (Performance), describe across all attributes; don't cluster on Online; don't read velocity as a raw scalar.  
- **"Why only 21 stores?"** → "Sandbox sample. Your instance runs all \~250."
